title: lythraceae
intro: The lythraceae rank is a family of flowering plants, commonly known as the loosestrife or fringe tree family. This family consists of approximately 220 species, widely distributed across the globe, and is characterized by their showy flowers and often woody or herbaceous growth habit. Lythraceae species can be found in various habitats, including wetlands, grasslands, and forests, and many are popular ornamental plants. The family name lythraceae comes from the Greek word 'lythron,' meaning 'little blood,' likely referring to the red color of some species' flowers.
